slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 8 - Computer Science
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: If a research scenario or project requires: **cpu-free I/O-memory transfer**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Group Theory Group
Full Adder
Set Theory Operations
Direct Memory Access (DMA)
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of cpu-free I/O-memory transfer, you should select **Direct Memory Access (DMA)**.

slides.question: Q2: If a research scenario or project requires: **instruction pipeline execution stalls**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Pipelining Hazards
Permutation
Equivalence Relation
Flip-Flops
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of instruction pipeline execution stalls, you should select **Pipelining Hazards**.

slides.question: Q3: If a research scenario or project requires: **fetch-decode-execute basic loop**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Instruction Cycle
Partially Ordered Set (Poset)
Boolean Algebra Minimization
Synchronous Counter
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of fetch-decode-execute basic loop, you should select **Instruction Cycle**.

slides.question: Q4: If a research scenario or project requires: **reduced uniform instruction set design**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Vertex Degree
Decoder
Karnaugh Map (K-map)
RISC Architecture
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of reduced uniform instruction set design, you should select **RISC Architecture**.

slides.question: Q5: If a research scenario or project requires: **floating-point binary representation format**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Euler Path
Von Neumann Architecture
IEEE 754 Standard
Multiplexer (MUX)
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of floating-point binary representation format, you should select **IEEE 754 Standard**.

slides.question: Q6: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Propositional Logic Connectives**?
options: It is correctly described as: are symbols used to connect propositions to form compound propositions, such as AND, OR, and NOT.
It has no relation to: compound proposition builders
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a closed loop in a graph that visits every vertex exactly once and returns to the starting vertex.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a combinational logic circuit that takes a single input line and routes it to one of several digital outputs.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Propositional Logic Connectives** is accurately described as: are symbols used to connect propositions to form compound propositions, such as AND, OR, and NOT.

slides.question: Q7: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Predicate Logic Quantifiers**?
options: It is correctly described as: are symbols like Universal (forall) and Existential (exists) that specify the scope of a predicate's variables.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a combinational circuit that adds three single-bit inputs (A, B, and Carry-in) and outputs Sum and Carry.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a set combined with a binary operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.
It has no relation to: predicate variable scoping symbols
slides.explanation: Correct! **Predicate Logic Quantifiers** is accurately described as: are symbols like Universal (forall) and Existential (exists) that specify the scope of a predicate's variables.

slides.question: Q8: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Set Theory Operations**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: are sequential logic circuits used as basic memory storage elements, changing states based on clock inputs (e.g. JK, D).
It has no relation to: set manipulation mathematical operations
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an ordered arrangement of all or part of a set of objects, where the order of selection matters.
It is correctly described as: include Union, Intersection, Difference, and Complement used to manipulate and combine collections of objects.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Set Theory Operations** is accurately described as: include Union, Intersection, Difference, and Complement used to manipulate and combine collections of objects.

slides.question: Q9: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Equivalence Relation**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a binary relation that is simultaneously reflexive, symmetric, and transitive, partitioning a set into equivalence classes.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the process of simplifying a Boolean expression to reduce the number of logic gates required for implementation.
It has no relation to: partitioning reflexive-symmetric-transitive relation
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a digital counter where all flip-flops are triggered simultaneously by a single common clock pulse.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Equivalence Relation** is accurately described as: is a binary relation that is simultaneously reflexive, symmetric, and transitive, partitioning a set into equivalence classes.

slides.question: Q10: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Partially Ordered Set (Poset)**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a graphical method used to simplify Boolean algebra expressions without using algebraic laws.
It has no relation to: reflexive-antisymmetric-transitive ordering framework
It is correctly described as: is a set combined with a binary relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a combinational circuit that converts an n-bit binary input code into a maximum of 2^n unique output lines.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Partially Ordered Set (Poset)** is accurately described as: is a set combined with a binary relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

slides.question: Q11: If a research scenario or project requires: **minimal kernel user-space service design**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Process Synchronization
Page Replacement Algorithm
Microkernel Architecture
Inter-Process Communication (IPC)
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of minimal kernel user-space service design, you should select **Microkernel Architecture**.

slides.question: Q12: If a research scenario or project requires: **multiprocessor cache synchronization**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Cache Coherence Protocols
I/O Buffering
Thrashing
Deadlock Prevention
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of multiprocessor cache synchronization, you should select **Cache Coherence Protocols**.

slides.question: Q13: If a research scenario or project requires: **logical module-based memory division**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Disk Scheduling
Banker's Algorithm
Memory Segmentation
Thread
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of logical module-based memory division, you should select **Memory Segmentation**.

slides.question: Q14: If a research scenario or project requires: **hardware-specific control translator program**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Paging
Device Driver
File Allocation Methods
Mutex
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of hardware-specific control translator program, you should select **Device Driver**.

slides.question: Q15: If a research scenario or project requires: **peripheral device print job spooler**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Spooling
Counting Semaphore
Virtual Memory
System Calls
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of peripheral device print job spooler, you should select **Spooling**.

slides.question: Q16: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Process States**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is the process of storing the state of a CPU process/thread so that it can be restored and resume execution later.
It is correctly described as: include Ready, Running, Waiting, New, and Terminated, representing the life-cycle stages of a process.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a hardware cache memory that stores recent translations of virtual memory addresses to physical memory addresses.
It has no relation to: process lifecycle stage indicators
slides.explanation: Correct! **Process States** is accurately described as: include Ready, Running, Waiting, New, and Terminated, representing the life-cycle stages of a process.

slides.question: Q17: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **CPU Scheduling**?
options: It is correctly described as: is the process of allocating CPU time to processes in the ready queue using algorithms like Round Robin or SJF.
It has no relation to: ready queue CPU allocation
It is fundamentally characterized by: decides which memory page to swap out of physical RAM when a new page needs to be allocated (e.g. LRU, FIFO).
It is equivalent in purpose to: refers to mechanisms (like pipes, message queues, shared memory) that allow processes to communicate and share data.
slides.explanation: Correct! **CPU Scheduling** is accurately described as: is the process of allocating CPU time to processes in the ready queue using algorithms like Round Robin or SJF.

slides.question: Q18: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Process Synchronization**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: uses temporary memory areas to store data during transfers between fast devices (like CPU) and slow I/O peripherals.
It has no relation to: prevention of race conditions
It is correctly described as: coordinates the execution of concurrent processes to ensure no two processes access shared data simultaneously.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a state where the operating system spends more time swapping pages in and out of disk than executing instructions.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Process Synchronization** is accurately described as: coordinates the execution of concurrent processes to ensure no two processes access shared data simultaneously.

slides.question: Q19: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Deadlock Prevention**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a strategy that eliminates at least one of the four necessary Coffman conditions (Mutual Exclusion, Hold & Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait).
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the smallest unit of execution within a process, sharing the process's memory space but maintaining its own stack and registers.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the process of managing I/O requests for a hard disk drive using algorithms like SCAN, C-SCAN, or SSTF.
It has no relation to: coffman condition elimination strategy
slides.explanation: Correct! **Deadlock Prevention** is accurately described as: is a strategy that eliminates at least one of the four necessary Coffman conditions (Mutual Exclusion, Hold & Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait).

slides.question: Q20: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Banker's Algorithm**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a mutual exclusion locking mechanism used to synchronize access to a resource, ensuring only one thread holds the lock at a time.
It is equivalent in purpose to: include Contiguous, Linked, and Indexed allocation strategies for storing files on physical storage devices.
It is correctly described as: is a resource allocation and deadlock avoidance algorithm that tests for safety by simulating allocation limits.
It has no relation to: safe-state resource allocation simulator
slides.explanation: Correct! **Banker's Algorithm** is accurately described as: is a resource allocation and deadlock avoidance algorithm that tests for safety by simulating allocation limits.

slides.question: Q21: If a research scenario or project requires: **derived-to-base class reuse mechanism**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Dynamic Programming
Inheritance
Breadth-First Search (BFS)
Doubly Linked List
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of derived-to-base class reuse mechanism, you should select **Inheritance**.

slides.question: Q22: If a research scenario or project requires: **data-hiding and modular unit bundling**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Encapsulation
Depth-First Search (DFS)
Hashing
Merge Sort
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of data-hiding and modular unit bundling, you should select **Encapsulation**.

slides.question: Q23: If a research scenario or project requires: **mathematical operation behavior model**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Big O Complexity
Heap Data Structure
Binary Search
Abstract Data Type (ADT)
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of mathematical operation behavior model, you should select **Abstract Data Type (ADT)**.

slides.question: Q24: If a research scenario or project requires: **automatic dead heap memory reclamation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: AVL Tree
Garbage Collection
Call Stack
Stack Data Structure
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of automatic dead heap memory reclamation, you should select **Garbage Collection**.

slides.question: Q25: If a research scenario or project requires: **name accessibility region code boundary**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Queue Data Structure
Pointer dereferencing
Quick Sort
Variable Scope
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of name accessibility region code boundary, you should select **Variable Scope**.

slides.question: Q26: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Binary Search Tree (BST)**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a linear collection of data nodes where each node points to the next node in the sequence.
It is correctly described as: is a node-based binary tree data structure where the left subtree contains keys less than the parent, and the right contains keys greater.
It has no relation to: O(log n) average search time
It is fundamentally characterized by: represents a graph as an array of linked lists, where each index represents a vertex and its list contains its adjacent neighbors.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Binary Search Tree (BST)** is accurately described as: is a node-based binary tree data structure where the left subtree contains keys less than the parent, and the right contains keys greater.

slides.question: Q27: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Dijkstra's Algorithm**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: traverses a graph level by level, utilizing a queue data structure to visit vertices in order of increasing distance.
It has no relation to: single-source shortest path optimization
It is correctly described as: finds the shortest path from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a weighted graph with non-negative edge weights.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a linear collection of nodes where each node contains pointers to both the next and the previous nodes.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Dijkstra's Algorithm** is accurately described as: finds the shortest path from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a weighted graph with non-negative edge weights.

slides.question: Q28: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Dynamic Programming**?
options: It is correctly described as: solves complex problems by breaking them down into simpler subproblems and storing the results of these subproblems to avoid redundant computing.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a stable, divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that splits arrays in halves, sorts them, and merges them back.
It is fundamentally characterized by: traverses a graph by exploring as deep as possible along each branch before backtracking, utilizing a stack.
It has no relation to: memoization and tabulation paradigm
slides.explanation: Correct! **Dynamic Programming** is accurately described as: solves complex problems by breaking them down into simpler subproblems and storing the results of these subproblems to avoid redundant computing.

slides.question: Q29: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Hashing**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is an efficient search algorithm that repeatedly divides a sorted search interval in half to locate a target.
It has no relation to: O(1) average lookup complexity
It is correctly described as: maps data of arbitrary size to fixed-size values using a mathematical function, enabling constant-time average insertions and lookups.
It is fundamentally characterized by: describes the asymptotic upper bound of an algorithm's execution time or space requirement in terms of input size.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Hashing** is accurately described as: maps data of arbitrary size to fixed-size values using a mathematical function, enabling constant-time average insertions and lookups.

slides.question: Q30: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Heap Data Structure**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a linear data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle for inserting and removing elements.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a stack memory structure that stores information about active subroutines and functions in a running program.
It has no relation to: efficient priority queue implementation
It is correctly described as: is a complete binary tree that satisfies the heap property, where the parent node key is compared with its children.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Heap Data Structure** is accurately described as: is a complete binary tree that satisfies the heap property, where the parent node key is compared with its children.

slides.question: Q31: If a research scenario or project requires: **precompiled database SQL package**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Lost Update Anomaly
SQL JOIN
Stored Procedure
Third Normal Form (3NF)
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of precompiled database SQL package, you should select **Stored Procedure**.

slides.question: Q32: If a research scenario or project requires: **row unique identifier constraint**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)
Primary Key
Relational Algebra
Dirty Read
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of row unique identifier constraint, you should select **Primary Key**.

slides.question: Q33: If a research scenario or project requires: **minimal row identifier set**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Candidate Key
ACID Properties
Non-Repeatable Read
Database View
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of minimal row identifier set, you should select **Candidate Key**.

slides.question: Q34: If a research scenario or project requires: **valid foreign-to-primary key reference state**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Foreign Key
NoSQL Database
Phantom Read
Referential Integrity
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of valid foreign-to-primary key reference state, you should select **Referential Integrity**.

slides.question: Q35: If a research scenario or project requires: **unnecessary duplicate data storage**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Data Redundancy
B+ Tree Index
Query Optimizer
ER Modeling
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of unnecessary duplicate data storage, you should select **Data Redundancy**.

slides.question: Q36: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **First Normal Form (1NF)**?
options: It is correctly described as: requires that domain values must be atomic, meaning attributes cannot contain multiple or nested values.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a concurrency control method that guarantees serializability by locking data items in growing and shrinking phases.
It has no relation to: atomic column values requirement
It is equivalent in purpose to: reverts all changes made during the current transaction if a failure occurs before completion, ensuring atomicity.
slides.explanation: Correct! **First Normal Form (1NF)** is accurately described as: requires that domain values must be atomic, meaning attributes cannot contain multiple or nested values.

slides.question: Q37: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Second Normal Form (2NF)**?
options: It has no relation to: elimination of partial dependencies
It is fundamentally characterized by: combines records from two or more tables in a relational database based on a related column between them.
It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when two concurrent transactions read and update the same data item, where one overwrite is lost.
It is correctly described as: requires a table to be in 1NF and ensure that all non-prime attributes are fully functionally dependent on the primary key.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Second Normal Form (2NF)** is accurately described as: requires a table to be in 1NF and ensure that all non-prime attributes are fully functionally dependent on the primary key.

slides.question: Q38: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Third Normal Form (3NF)**?
options: It has no relation to: elimination of transitive dependencies
It is correctly described as: requires a table to be in 2NF and ensure that no non-prime attribute is transitively dependent on the primary key.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a procedural query language consisting of operations like Select, Project, Join, and Union to manipulate relations.
It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when a transaction reads data that has been modified by another concurrent transaction but not yet committed.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Third Normal Form (3NF)** is accurately described as: requires a table to be in 2NF and ensure that no non-prime attribute is transitively dependent on the primary key.

slides.question: Q39: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)**?
options: It has no relation to: eliminating overlapping candidate key anomalies
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a virtual table based on the result-set of an active SQL statement, containing no physical data of its own.
It is correctly described as: is a stronger version of 3NF where for every non-trivial functional dependency X -> Y, X must be a superkey.
It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when a transaction re-reads data it previously read and finds that another committed transaction has modified it.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)** is accurately described as: is a stronger version of 3NF where for every non-trivial functional dependency X -> Y, X must be a superkey.

slides.question: Q40: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **ACID Properties**?
options: It has no relation to: transaction reliability standards
It is correctly described as: guarantee database transaction reliability: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability.
It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when a transaction re-runs a query returning a set of rows and finds new rows added by another committed transaction.
It is fundamentally characterized by: provides a mechanism for storage and retrieval of data modeled in formats other than tabular relations (e.g. document, key-value).
slides.explanation: Correct! **ACID Properties** is accurately described as: guarantee database transaction reliability: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability.

slides.question: Q41: If a research scenario or project requires: **inter-module interdependence indicator**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Firewall
CI/CD Pipeline
Coupling
Subnet Mask
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of inter-module interdependence indicator, you should select **Coupling**.

slides.question: Q42: If a research scenario or project requires: **software development process model**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Border Gateway Protocol (BGP)
SDLC Model
Black-box Testing
IPv6 Address Space
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of software development process model, you should select **SDLC Model**.

slides.question: Q43: If a research scenario or project requires: **standardized software visualization diagrams**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Agile Methodology
UML Diagrams
White-box Testing
Symmetric Encryption
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of standardized software visualization diagrams, you should select **UML Diagrams**.

slides.question: Q44: If a research scenario or project requires: **formal software requirements specifications**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Asymmetric Encryption
Design Pattern
SRS Document
Software Architecture
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of formal software requirements specifications, you should select **SRS Document**.

slides.question: Q45: If a research scenario or project requires: **improving code quality without behavior change**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Refactoring
Scrum Framework
DNS (Domain Name System)
Unit Testing
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of improving code quality without behavior change, you should select **Refactoring**.

slides.question: Q46: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **OSI Model**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a protocol used to map an Internet Protocol (IP) address to a physical machine Media Access Control (MAC) address.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a distributed version control system designed to track changes in source code during software development.
It has no relation to: 7-layer abstraction framework
It is correctly described as: is a conceptual framework of 7 layers that standardizes telecommunication and networking protocol functions.
slides.explanation: Correct! **OSI Model** is accurately described as: is a conceptual framework of 7 layers that standardizes telecommunication and networking protocol functions.

slides.question: Q47: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **TCP/IP Protocol**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: automates software delivery phases: building, testing, and deploying changes to production environments.
It is correctly described as: is a suite of communication protocols used to interconnect network devices on the internet, emphasizing reliable delivery.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a network security device that monitors and filters incoming and outgoing network traffic based on established security rules.
It has no relation to: reliable internet transport framework
slides.explanation: Correct! **TCP/IP Protocol** is accurately described as: is a suite of communication protocols used to interconnect network devices on the internet, emphasizing reliable delivery.

slides.question: Q48: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Subnet Mask**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: provides 128-bit addresses to replace 32-bit IPv4 addresses, accommodating the massive growth of internet devices.
It has no relation to: IP address segmentation tool
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a method of software testing that examines the functionality of an application without peering into its internal structures.
It is correctly described as: is a 32-bit number that masks an IP address, dividing the IP address into network address and host address portions.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Subnet Mask** is accurately described as: is a 32-bit number that masks an IP address, dividing the IP address into network address and host address portions.

slides.question: Q49: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Border Gateway Protocol (BGP)**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a method of software testing that tests internal structures or workings of an application, requiring code visibility.
It is fundamentally characterized by: promotes continuous iteration of development and testing throughout the software development life cycle.
It has no relation to: internet autonomous routing protocol
It is correctly described as: is a standardized exterior gateway protocol designed to exchange routing and reachability information among autonomous systems.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Border Gateway Protocol (BGP)** is accurately described as: is a standardized exterior gateway protocol designed to exchange routing and reachability information among autonomous systems.

slides.question: Q50: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Symmetric Encryption**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a reusable, template-like solution to a commonly occurring design problem in software architecture.
It is correctly described as: uses the exact same key for both the encryption of plaintext and the decryption of ciphertext.
It has no relation to: single shared-key cryptography
It is equivalent in purpose to: refers to the high-level structures of a software system, the discipline of creating such structures.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Symmetric Encryption** is accurately described as: uses the exact same key for both the encryption of plaintext and the decryption of ciphertext.

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 8. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Computer Science Mock Exam 8.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Computer Science questions!
